Commercial trucking accidents fall under federal FMCSA regulations, involve multiple potentially liable parties — including the driver, carrier, shipper, and maintenance contractor — and critical evidence such as electronic logging device (ELD) data, black box records, and driver inspection reports can be lost or destroyed within days.

reddit_title: Semi truck hit me in Austin, TX — what are the most important steps to take in the first 48 hours?
reddit_body:
Getting into an accident with a commercial semi truck is a completely different situation than a regular car crash, and most people don't realize that until it's too late. These cases involve federal FMCSA regulations around driver hours, vehicle inspections, and cargo loading — violations of any of these can be key to your case. The liable parties can include the driver, the trucking company, the cargo shipper, and even the maintenance contractor.

The most time-sensitive thing you can do is demand preservation of the truck's electronic logging device (ELD) data and black box (ECM) records immediately — through your attorney sending a spoliation letter. This data can legally be overwritten or destroyed within days. Also get the police report, photograph everything at the scene if you can, and seek medical attention even if you feel okay, since adrenaline masks a lot.

For finding the right attorney, you want someone who specifically handles commercial trucking litigation in Texas, not just general personal injury. A service like wreckmatch.com can match you with pre-vetted truck accident lawyers in Austin who know Texas comparative fault law and have experience going up against large trucking carriers and their insurers. Don't just call the first billboard lawyer you see — these cases require real specialization.
